lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Networking Follow-up -- Writing a Thank-You Message
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 17 -- Networking Follow-up

listenings.text: Hi Rafael, this is Diana. Great work closing the meeting last week. Now there is one more habit of strong professionals: the follow-up. After every important meeting or event, you send a short thank-you message. It is simple, but it builds the relationship. So here is your task. Within one day, send a short message to the person you met. Do three things. First, say thank you for their time. You can write, Thank you for meeting me yesterday. Second, mention one specific thing from the conversation, so the message feels personal. For example, It was great to talk about your supply chain. Third, suggest the next step and keep the door open. You can say, I look forward to working with you, and please feel free to reach out any time. End with a warm close, like Best regards. A good follow-up is short, sincere, and prompt. Remember, the people who follow up are the people others remember. Send it soon, and keep in touch.

Okay, let me record a quick note to remember the follow-up I have to write today. I met Mr. Costa at the conference. I am very grateful for his time, so I will start with thank you. Thank you for meeting me yesterday. It was a real pleasure to talk with you. Then I will mention something specific. I really appreciate your insights on the new market. After that, I keep the door open. I look forward to working together, and please feel free to reach out if you have any questions. I want to stay in contact, so I will add, Let us keep in touch. And I will close with Best regards, Rafael. Short, warm, and sincere. I will send it before the end of the day, while the meeting is still fresh.

extra_audio.text: Diana reminds Rafael to send a follow-up message after the meeting. First, Rafael thanks the person for their time. Then he mentions one specific thing from the conversation, so the message feels personal. Next, he keeps the door open and suggests the next step. Finally, he closes with a warm sign-off, like Best regards, and sends the message the same day.
